subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Supported player range** — The rulebook states **2 to 5 players**.
- **Explicit variant found** — **Double-routes change in 2-3 player games**.
- **Exact rule text effect** — In **2 or 3 players**, only one track of each double-route can be used; the other closes.
- **No separate setup scaling found** — Starting hand, trains, and initial tickets are the same for 2-5 players.

#### Boards & Core Pieces
- **Map board** — 1 board map of North American train routes.
- **Train cars total** — 240 colored train cars in box.
- **Per playable color** — 45 each in Blue, Red, Green, Yellow, and Black.
- **Extra pieces** — Replacement cars are included in each color.
- **Scoring markers** — 5 wooden scoring markers (1 per player color).

#### Cards (144 illustrated cards total)
- **Train Car deck** — 110 Train Car cards.
- **Color sets** — 12 each of Box, Passenger, Tanker, Reefer, Freight, Hopper, Coal, Caboose.
- **Wild cards** — 14 Locomotives.
- **Destination deck** — 30 Destination Ticket cards.
- **Reference card** — 1 Summary card.
- **Bonus card** — 1 Longest Continuous Path bonus card.
- **Promo cards** — 1 additional-maps promo card, 1 Days of Wonder promo card.

#### Other Included Items
- **Rulebook** — 1 rules booklet.
- **Online code** — 1 Days of Wonder online access number.

#### Fast Count Check Before Teaching
- **Train deck check** — 110 Train Car cards ready.
- **Ticket deck check** — 30 Destination Tickets ready.
- **Player kit check** — Each player color has 45 usable trains + 1 matching marker.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Layout Sequence
1. **Place board** — Put the North America map in the center.
2. **Place bonus card** — Put Longest Continuous Path face up near board edge.
3. **Prepare train market** — Shuffle Train Car deck, place face down, reveal 5 face-up cards.
4. **Prepare ticket deck** — Shuffle Destination Tickets and place face down.
5. **Reserve a discard area** — Leave clear space for spent Train Car cards.
6. **Reserve player space** — Leave hand space for each player’s cards and train supply.

#### Visibility Priorities
- **Public information** — Board routes, score track, face-up train cards, bonus card.
- **Hidden information** — Destination Tickets in hand remain secret until final scoring.
- **Practical placement** — Keep face-up train row close to draw pile for fast replenishment.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Setup (All Player Counts)
- **Color choice** — Each player takes 1 color set of 45 trains + matching scoring marker.
- **Score marker start** — Place marker on **Start (0)** on the score track.
- **Train cards dealt** — Deal **4 Train Car cards** to each player.
- **Destination tickets dealt** — Deal **3 Destination Tickets** to each player.
- **Mandatory keep** — Keep at least **2** of the 3 starting tickets.
- **Optional keep** — A player may keep all 3.
- **Returned tickets** — Put returned tickets on bottom of Destination deck.

#### Hidden/Hand Rules
- **Ticket secrecy** — Destination Tickets stay hidden until endgame reveal.
- **Train card hand limit** — No limit.
- **Destination ticket limit** — No limit.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Turn Start
- **First player rule** — Most experienced traveler goes first.
- **Direction of play** — Continue clockwise.
- **Turn restriction** — On each turn, choose exactly one action type.
- **Action types** — Draw Train Car Cards, Claim a Route, or Draw Destination Tickets.
- **No opening draft phase** — The game starts immediately with normal turn actions.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Turn Sequence
1. **Choose one action** — Draw Train Car Cards, Claim a Route, or Draw Destination Tickets.
2. **Resolve fully** — Complete all restrictions of that one action.
3. **Score if needed** — If you claimed a route, score immediately from route table.
4. **Check end trigger** — If you end turn with 0, 1, or 2 trains left, trigger final round.
5. **Pass turn** — Play continues clockwise.

#### Final-Round Sequence
1. **Trigger condition met** — A player ends turn at 0-2 trains.
2. **One last turn each** — Every player, including trigger player, gets one final turn.
3. **Game end** — Proceed to final scoring.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action A — Draw Train Car Cards
- **Base draw count** — Draw 2 Train Car cards.
- **Draw sources** — Face-up row or blind top-deck draw.
- **Refill timing** — If you take a face-up card, refill immediately before next draw decision.

#### Locomotive Rules While Drawing
- **Wild identity** — Locomotives are multi-color wilds for route claims.
- **Face-up first draw** — If your first draw is a face-up Locomotive, turn ends (no second draw).
- **Replacement restriction** — If replacement after first draw is a Locomotive, you cannot take it as second draw.
- **Blind locomotive** — A blind-drawn Locomotive still counts as only one draw; second draw is allowed.

#### Face-Up Row Reset Rule
- **Reset trigger** — If 3 of 5 face-up cards are Locomotives.
- **Reset effect** — Discard all 5 and reveal 5 new face-up cards.

#### Deck Exhaustion Rule
- **Normal refill** — If draw deck empties, shuffle discards into a new draw pile.
- **Rare no-cards case** — If draw pile and discard are both empty, you cannot take Draw action this turn.

#### Action B — Claim a Route
- **Choose route** — Select one open route between adjacent cities.
- **Card payment** — Play cards equal to route length.
- **Color requirement** — All cards in the set must match one color/type (Locomotives may substitute).
- **Colored routes** — Must match printed route color.
- **Gray routes** — Any single color set works.
- **Piece placement** — Place one train on each space of the claimed route.
- **Discard spent cards** — Put used Train Car cards in discard pile.
- **Immediate scoring** — Score by route length: 1→1, 2→2, 3→4, 4→7, 5→10, 6→15.

#### Route Claim Limits
- **One route per turn** — Never more than one claimed route in a turn.
- **Connectivity not required** — Route may be disconnected from your network.
- **Single-player ownership on doubles** — One player may not claim both tracks of a double-route pair.

#### Player-Count Rule for Double-Routes
--- 2 Players ---
- **Double-route availability** — 1 track of each double-route may be used total.
- **Closure rule** — Once 1 track is claimed, the parallel track is closed.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Double-route availability** — 1 track of each double-route may be used total.
- **Closure rule** — Once 1 track is claimed, the parallel track is closed.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Double-route availability** — Both of the 2 parallel tracks are available (subject to normal ownership rule).

--- 5 Players ---
- **Double-route availability** — Both of the 2 parallel tracks are available (subject to normal ownership rule).

#### Action C — Draw Destination Tickets
- **Draw count** — Draw 3 Destination Tickets from top of deck (or fewer if deck is low).
- **Mandatory keep** — Keep at least 1.
- **Optional keep** — Keep 2 or 3.
- **Return flow** — Return unkept tickets to bottom of deck.
- **Secrecy** — Kept tickets remain hidden until final scoring.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Destination Ticket Resolution
- **Ticket structure** — Each ticket names 2 cities and a point value.
- **Completion scoring** — Add points if your routes connect those cities.
- **Failure scoring** — Subtract points if not connected.
- **Information state** — Tickets stay secret until final reveal.

#### Longest Continuous Path Bonus
- **Bonus value** — +10 points.
- **Measurement rule** — Count one continuous line of your trains.
- **Loop allowance** — Loops are allowed.
- **City revisits** — Passing through same city multiple times is allowed.
- **No segment reuse** — A specific plastic train segment cannot be used twice in same measured path.
- **Tie handling** — All tied players receive +10.

#### Scoring Timing Split
- **During game** — Route claims score immediately.
- **Endgame only** — Destination Tickets and Longest Path bonus are resolved at final scoring.

#### Player-Count Reminder
--- 2 Players ---
- **Double-route pressure** — Chokepoints are harsher because only 1 side of each double-route is usable.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Double-route pressure** — Chokepoints are harsher because only 1 side of each double-route is usable.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Double-route pressure** — Normal access to both 2-track doubles increases reroute options.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Double-route pressure** — Normal access to both 2-track doubles increases reroute options.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Endgame Trigger
- **Trigger condition** — A player ends their turn with 0, 1, or 2 trains left.
- **Final round rule** — Every player, including trigger player, takes one final turn.
- **Then stop** — After those turns, game ends immediately.

#### Final Scoring Sequence
1. **Verify route points** — Confirm in-game route scoring totals.
2. **Reveal all tickets** — Show Destination Tickets for every player.
3. **Score tickets** — Add completed ticket values and subtract incomplete ticket values.
4. **Award longest path** — Give +10 to all players tied for longest legal continuous path.
5. **Compare totals** — Highest total score wins.

#### All Point Sources
- **Route points (in game)** — Length 1=1, 2=2, 3=4, 4=7, 5=10, 6=15.
- **Destination points (endgame)** — Add completed ticket values.
- **Destination penalties (endgame)** — Subtract incomplete ticket values.
- **Bonus points (endgame)** — +10 Longest Continuous Path.

#### Tiebreakers
1. **First tiebreaker** — Most completed Destination Tickets.
2. **Second tiebreaker** — Player with Longest Continuous Path card.
3. **If still tied** — Shared victory by table agreement.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Checklist
- **Ticket overlap first** — Identify shared corridors across your starting tickets before turn 1.
- **Chokepoint priority** — Flag routes that would be hard to replace if blocked.
- **Color plan** — Decide which 2-3 colors matter most for your first claims.

#### Player-Count Opening Priorities
--- 2 Players ---
- **Route urgency** — Blocking pressure is moderate, but each double-route is effectively 1-use, so secure key parallels early.
- **Ticket timing** — You can often delay extra ticket draws until your first 1-2 backbone links are complete.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Route urgency** — The 1-use double-route rule makes contested links very sharp; claim must-have links earlier.
- **Locomotive value** — Save locomotives for 1-2 hard reroutes, not convenience claims.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Route urgency** — Competition rises and both tracks on 2-track doubles are available; still secure map bottlenecks quickly.
- **Tempo line** — Mix 1 early claim with 1 draw-heavy turn to stay flexible.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Route urgency** — Board congestion is highest; claim 1-2 critical connectors earlier than you think.
- **Pace control** — In 5-player rounds, reassess ticket completion after each full table cycle to avoid late dead turns.

#### High-Value Early Habits
- **Long-route efficiency** — Prefer 4-6 length routes when safe; they are efficient per turn.
- **Wild-card discipline** — Hold locomotives for contested or color-tight routes.
- **Clock awareness** — Monitor opponents’ remaining trains to avoid getting caught with unfinished tickets.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1 — Drawing extra tickets too early
- **Why it hurts** — Unfinished tickets become direct negative points.
- **Fix** — Draw more tickets only after your core network is reliable.

#### Mistake 2 — Misplaying double-routes by player count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Error** — Planning around both tracks of a double-route.
- **Rule reality** — Only 1 of the 2 tracks is usable total.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Error** — Assuming a parallel track remains available.
- **Rule reality** — Once 1 side is claimed, the other is closed.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Error** — Forgetting both 2 tracks can be used by different players.
- **Fix** — Recalculate opponent pathing when 1 side gets taken.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Error** — Waiting too long on key doubles due to high congestion from 5 players.
- **Fix** — Claim 1 critical double sooner even at lower immediate score.

#### Mistake 3 — Burning locomotives on easy routes
- **Why it hurts** — You lose flexibility for blocked or color-tight routes later.
- **Fix** — Spend locomotives where replacement lines are weak.

#### Mistake 4 — Overtrusting the visible score track
- **Why it hurts** — Hidden ticket failures can reverse standings at final reveal.
- **Fix** — Track your own completion certainty, not just current points.

#### Mistake 5 — Triggering endgame without verification
- **Why it hurts** — You may lock in unfinished high-penalty tickets.
- **Fix** — Before pushing to 0-2 trains, check every live ticket path and final-turn backup.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1 — Claim now vs draw now
- **Claim now** — Best when a route is likely to be blocked by next turn cycle.
- **Draw now** — Best when your plan has multiple legal route options and needs card quality.
- **Practical test** — Ask: "If I wait one round, can I still connect this ticket with a reasonable backup?"

#### Decision 2 — Long points vs ticket certainty
- **Long-route upside** — Better point efficiency and faster score growth.
- **Ticket-risk downside** — Unfinished tickets can erase that gain.
- **Best use** — Prioritize long routes that also advance at least one ticket connection.

#### Decision 3 — Expand ticket portfolio vs consolidate network
- **Expand** — Draw tickets when your current map has many attach points.
- **Consolidate** — Keep building when current tickets still need expensive bridges.

#### Decision 4 — Denial move vs self-development
- **Denial move value** — High if it blocks a likely opponent line and helps your own network.
- **Denial trap** — Low if it costs a full turn without improving your own ticket completion odds.

metadata.notes: Written from provided Ticket to Ride rulebook text (2015 reprint excerpt) as primary source.
